_doc: EXAMPLE config for the M10 autonomous shadow supervisor (src/autonomous_shadow_supervisor.py). PLACEHOLDER, not a secret and not a real internal host. Copy to a local (gitignored) config and edit for your own endpoint. The default dry-run / fake-endpoint path needs none of the live fields.

endpoint._doc: Local OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Targets Agents-A1 GGUF-style serving (llama.cpp / llama-swap / vLLM / Ollama-OpenAI shim). The supervisor POSTs {base_url}/chat/completions.
endpoint._model_doc: A GGUF chat endpoint returns OUTPUT TEXT ONLY — no router logits. So live tasks have NO telemetry feature row: the supervisor sets telemetry_missing=true and policy=null. It NEVER fabricates features.
endpoint._api_key_doc: Most local endpoints ignore this. Do NOT put a real secret here; use an env var in your own local config if your endpoint needs auth.

task_sources._doc: PUBLIC fixture task queue — safe to use for committed samples and tests.
OPTIONAL local private queue — gitignored, real prompts, NEVER committed. Absent by default.

features._doc: Optional telemetry feature rows keyed by prompt_id. When a row is missing for a task, telemetry_missing=true and policy=null.

verifiers._doc: Toggle cheap verifier adapters (src/verifiers.py). Each produces an auto_outcome signal only; none is gold.

escalation._doc: When to flag a task for later HUMAN review. Escalation is advisory; it never sets a human outcome.

run._doc: Runtime defaults. dry-run uses the deterministic fake output (no network); live calls the endpoint above.
run._log_doc: Default log path is under the gitignored private dir — raw prompt/output text stays local. Commit only aggregate-only or redacted artifacts.
